summary: Gruber analyzes the expected supply constraints for iPhone X at launch, drawing parallels to past Apple products like AirPods and Apple Watch that shipped late with limited availability. He walks through the likely timeline for review units and embargo dates, concluding that pre-orders will open before reviews publish. This creates a dilemma for buyers: pre-order blind or wait for reviews and potentially not receive the phone until 2018. Gruber raises legitimate open questions about Face ID, the home button replacement, and OLED quality, acknowledging that negative answers to these could spell disaster. He suggests the practical move is to pre-order and cancel if reviews disappoint, or resell on the gray market.
key_points: Late Apple products always have constrained supply at launch — AirPods and Apple Watch followed the same pattern
The review embargo will almost certainly lift after pre-orders begin, forcing buyers to decide without full information
Key unknowns about Face ID, the gesture system, Control Center placement, and OLED quality make this a higher-stakes pre-order than usual
Supply could be so tight that waiting even minutes past midnight could push delivery dates to December or January
China's demand for iPhone X as a status item will further strain already limited global supply
money_quotes: When an Apple product is late, supply is constrained when it ships. The two go hand-in-hand.
If you wait until you read the reviews the next week, you might be waiting until January for delivery.
The reviews of iPhone X will be the most anticipated since the reviews of the original iPhone in 2007.
Does Face ID really work so well that you won't miss Touch ID? Is the swipe from bottom gesture really a good replacement for the home button?
If the answer to any of these questions is no, it's a problem. If the answer to several of these questions is no, it's a disaster.
However the reviews turn out, the gray market for iPhone X is going to be insane.
I can speculate about these dates now because I don't have an iPhone X review unit. But once I do, I'll be under an NDA and won't be able to talk about it.
key_insight: iPhone X's supply constraints will force consumers into the unusual position of having to pre-order Apple's most radically redesigned iPhone in a decade before any reviews are published.
